response.justification: Candidate has strong senior-level software engineering credentials and some dated C experience (2014-2015), but fundamentally mismatches the role's core requirements. Missing native-level Japanese proficiency (required), no embedded firmware or Linux kernel expertise, zero networking/security domain knowledge (MACsec, 802.1X, switch architectures), and C skills are outdated and never applied in embedded/UNIX contexts. Background is entirely web/mobile/data applications; this role requires immediate productivity in specialized embedded LAN-switch security development.
strengths: 15+ years senior/staff-level software development experience with demonstrated ability to lead and mentor across multiple domains and languages
Deep expertise in software craftsmanship, TDD, CI/CD, modern development practices, and infrastructure (Docker, Kubernetes) showing systematic engineering discipline
Full-stack technical versatility and track record of quickly learning new domains (moved from backend to iOS/tvOS at WatchUp, contributed across web/mobile/embedded systems)
concerns: No native-level Japanese language proficiency; only English (Fluent), Romanian, and basic German—role explicitly requires native Japanese and this is a stated requirement, not negotiable for Japanese embedded systems company

C programming experience exists but is outdated (2014-2015 at National Instruments) and was never in UNIX/Linux embedded contexts; no Linux kernel source-code modification experience shown, which is required

Zero background in embedded networking/security domain: no experience with MACsec, IEEE 802.1X, NETCONF, SNMP, switch products, firmware development, or hardware-level security implementations required for this role

key_factors.factor: Embedded firmware and Linux kernel expertise gap
C programming skills outdated and not in embedded/UNIX domain
